expectations.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: File TDD-vendor-inventory-sync.md exists in outputs/ directory.

expectations.text: Document is at least 1500 words
expectations.evidence: Document spans 828 lines with substantive content across 14 sections; well over 1500 words.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Problem statement' explains stale inventory and $80K/month oversold incidents.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
expectations.evidence: Section 2 has 'In scope (this project)' and 'Explicitly out of scope (per PRD)' subsections.

expectations.text: Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
expectations.evidence: Section '3. High-level approach' with pipeline diagram and key shape decisions.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
expectations.evidence: Section '4. Detailed design' covers components, ingestion adapters, parsing, normalization, diff, sanity gates, apply, and staleness detection.

expectations.text: Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale for each
expectations.evidence: Section 7 table lists 7 alternatives (one adapter per vendor, Step Functions/Airflow, direct DB pull, no S3 staging, manual paste, asking Vendor K to switch to SFTP, webhook ingress) each with rejection rationale.

expectations.text: Contains an NFRs section with concrete targets (numbers/SLOs)
expectations.evidence: Section 8 NFRs with concrete numbers: 99.5% availability, <=15 min REST freshness, <=30 min SFTP/email, <$300/mo cost, 140K SKUs capacity.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
expectations.evidence: Section '9. Failure modes and how the design handles them' has detailed table of 15+ failure scenarios.

expectations.text: Contains a 'Rollout' / 'Migration' section, addressing the shadow-mode period from the PRD
expectations.evidence: Section 10 'Rollout and migration' with Phase 3 dedicated to '2 weeks from PRD' shadow mode and rollback strategy.

expectations.text: Contains an 'Observability' / 'Operations' section
expectations.evidence: Section 8.6 covers Observability (Datadog metrics, logs, tracing, alerts) and Section 11 covers Operations (runbooks, reconciliation, weekly digest).

expectations.text: Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
expectations.evidence: Section 13 'Open questions' has 11 numbered items including catalog API surface, shadow-mode arbitration, Vendor F rate-limit strategy.

expectations.text: No fabricated/invented URLs to internal systems
expectations.evidence: Only generic placeholders like s3://inventory-sync-raw/, vendor-k-inventory@inventory.ourdomain.com, and REST paths like /catalog/inventory; no fabricated internal URLs.

expectations.text: TDD is structured as a scheduled pull-based processing design
expectations.evidence: Section 3 explicitly states 'Pull-only' approach with Celery Beat scheduler driving per-vendor cadence; pipeline stages from ingest to apply.

expectations.text: Explicitly acknowledges and handles all 3 ingestion mechanisms (REST, SFTP, email) — does not collapse them into 'all REST'
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2 has distinct subsections 4.2.1 REST adapter (8 vendors), 4.2.2 SFTP adapter (3 vendors), 4.2.3 Email adapter (Vendor K); each with tailored design.

expectations.text: Proposes a per-vendor connector / adapter pattern (or equivalent abstraction) with shared core
expectations.evidence: Defines IngestionAdapter Protocol with three implementations; 'Three ingestion adapters, one downstream pipeline. REST/SFTP/Email adapters differ only in how they produce a raw payload... Everything after that is shared code.'

expectations.text: Calls out the Vendor F 60 req/hour rate limit and how the design respects it
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2.1: 'Vendor F's 60 req/hour cap means we have to choose... Default plan: 2x daily full pulls for Vendor F, gives us ~30 reqs each, comfortably under cap with headroom for retries.' Plus per-vendor token bucket in Redis.

expectations.text: Discusses per-vendor cursor / watermark / 'since' state, and how it's stored and recovered
expectations.evidence: VendorRun table stores per-run state and raw_s3_keys; pagination 'streamed to S3 page-by-page... vendor's pagination state is recoverable'; Open Q5 asks about updated_since support; payload_dedupe ledger by SHA-256.

expectations.text: Addresses no-overlap / locking between scheduled runs
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2.1: 'Per-vendor concurrency: at most 1 in-flight run per vendor (enforced via SELECT FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED on VendorRun or a Redis lock).' Also failure-modes row on operator double-trigger.

expectations.text: Defines a freshness SLO and per-vendor stale-data alerting (24h+ stale = alarm)
expectations.evidence: Section 2 sets concrete SLOs (<=15 min REST, <=30 min SFTP/email); Section 4.8 Staleness detection runs every 15 min comparing against vendor.staleness_threshold (default 24h); Section 8.6 alerts sev2 when vendor_freshness_seconds > 24h.

expectations.text: Addresses storage and rotation of per-vendor credentials
expectations.evidence: Section 8.5 Security: 'Vendor credentials in AWS Secrets Manager, fetched at task start, never written to logs. config JSONB stores references to secrets, not secret values. SFTP: prefer key-based auth; keys provisioned per vendor.'

expectations.text: Has a concrete plan for the emailed-CSV vendor (e.g., dedicated mailbox, automated parser, quarantine for malformed)
expectations.evidence: Section 4.2.3: dedicated mailbox vendor-k-inventory@inventory.ourdomain.com via SES inbound, SNS->SQS->Celery, acceptance rules (from-domain allowlist, subject regex, attachment types), quarantine bucket for failures with ops Slack ping, manual override path; ClamAV scanning.

expectations.text: Designs the shadow-mode rollout phase (compare to current source of truth, dispute resolution)
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 details 2-week shadow mode: 'Daily comparison report: today, new system would have changed X SKUs; Google Sheet recorded Y changes; here are the deltas. Ops reviews.' Plus apply_enabled config flag and Open Q2 for arbitration.

expectations.text: Designs the audit log of inventory changes with source and timestamp
expectations.evidence: inventory_audit table: 'Append-only audit of every change actually pushed to catalog' with run_id, vendor_id, sku, old_on_hand, new_on_hand, applied_at, source_observed_at. 2-year retention; DB user has INSERT, SELECT only.
